refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-bandwidth, low-latency network infrastructure.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and alerting system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network infrastructure.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data for adaptation.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes availability of accurate real-time packet loss data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP3 (real-time monitoring) tightly with DP1 to enhance adaptive protocol effectiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication components responsible for teleoperation control and communication quality management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and packet loss status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and tasks.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily hardware-focused; low containment relevance.
High containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not manage communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with NGHE Solution for overall system coordination.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface definitions and integration testing between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if adaptive protocol logic is partially implemented outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Strengthen integration interfaces between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety alert propagation.
Consider co-locating real-time monitoring components to reduce latency.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and verification criteria.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant stakeholders.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; no immediate revision needed.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
open_risks: Latency impact of retransmission on teleoperation responsiveness.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Performance test results under simulated network conditions.
Integration test reports between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
